source.section: Part 12 - Datalink & IFF > IFF from AZ/EL Page > Auto IFF (AZ/EL)

steps.action: Power Up IFF (Identify-Friend-or-Foe) and Datalink systems as shown previously in the MANUAL IFF example.
steps.voiced: Power up IFF and Datalink as shown in the Manual IFF procedure.

steps.action: Set AZ/EL Sub-page to IFF RDR.
steps.voiced: Set the AZ/EL Sub-page to IFF RDR.

steps.action: Select desired IFF scanning azimuth (in deg).
steps.voiced: Select the desired IFF scanning azimuth.

steps.action: Select desired IFF interrogation range (in nm).
steps.voiced: Select the desired IFF interrogation range.

steps.action: Make sure the L+S INT (Launch & Steering Interrogation) option is unboxed (de-selected).
Select AUTO INT (Automatic Interrogation) option. When selected, it will be boxed.
steps.voiced: Select AUTO INT — confirm it boxes.

steps.action: The IFF system will automatically perform scan interrogations at the selected azimuth centered about boresight every 10 seconds. AUTO IFF TUTORIAL (AZ/EL) CIT FOV (Field-of-View): shows the azimuthal extent of manual and automatic interrogations made by the CIT (combined interrogator/transponder ).
steps.voiced: Confirm IFF auto-interrogates at the selected azimuth every 10 seconds.
